data.title: NaaS Technology receives Nasdaq non-compliance notification
data.description:
• NaaS Technology (NASDAQ:NAAS) said on Friday that it has received notification from Nasdaq stating that the company is currently not in compliance with the minimum market value of listing securities. 
  • Nasdaq calculates the MVLS based upon the most recent total shares outstanding multiplied by the closing bid price. 
  • The MVLS Notice indicated that the company has 180 days, or until December 10, 2025, to regain compliance by having its MVLS close at US$35 million or more for a minimum of ten consecutive business days. 
  • Receipt of the MVLS notice does not result in the immediate delisting of the company's Class A ordinary shares, and has no immediate effect on the listing or the trading of the company's Class A ordinary shares on the Nasdaq under the symbol "NAAS.”

data.title: Exagen announces resale of up to 1.15M shares
data.description:
• Exagen (NASDAQ:XGN) is registering up to 1.15M shares of common stock for resale by Perceptive Credit Holdings or its affiliates.
  • The shares are issuable upon exercise of a warrant granted under a credit agreement dated April 25, 2025, with vesting tied to specific borrowing milestones.
  • Exagen will not receive proceeds from the resale.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: SA Asks: What lies ahead for the solar industry?
data.description:
The solar industry has been facing significant headwinds from fluctuating trade policies and proposed changes for certain tax credits under the Trump-backed spending bill making its way through Congress.

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Oliver Rodzianko and Victor Dergunov for their takes on what lies ahead for this emerging sector. 

Oliver Rodzianko: Residential-focused solar stocks like Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) and Enphase (NASDAQ:ENPH) face the most near-term pain due to the abrupt elimination of residential investment tax credits, or ITCs, while utility-scale firms like First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) and Array Technologies (NASDAQ:ARRY) face more manageable risks due to the phasing out of ITCs. 

I'm a First Solar (NASDAQ:FSLR) shareholder, and I'm treating my investment as a short-term cyclical play. Early phasing out of utility-scale ITCs actually means there could be more near-term demand as developers rush to lock in favorable prices with producers. This is a time to readjust take-profit targets and to not forget the inherent cyclicality of solar power investment returns.

In the long run, of course, solar power will continue to dominate the energy landscape with strong and growing demand, but that trajectory will be volatile, aligning with shifting policy and the macroeconomic climate.

Victor Dergunov: The solar industry is in a lot of trouble here. Rates remain high with no easing in sight. High home improvement loan rates are keeping demand less than optimal. In addition, tariffs are impacting margins, and the U.S. government's phase out of solar tax credits will further impact demand moving forward. 

We need lower interest rates, but we are already seeing prominent bankruptcies in the solar segment and more will likely materialize. Many companies may not make it, but the ones that remain may grow to dominate the solar industry in future years and decades. Several companies I like in this space include First Solar (FSLR), Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Enphase (NASDAQ:ENPH).

data.title: 180 Life Sciences announces $500M mixed shelf offering
data.description:
• 180 Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATNF) has filed to offer up to $500M in securities, including common stock, preferred stock, debt, warrants, and units, in one or more future offerings.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Circle Internet stock extends rally after landing Buy rating from Seaport
data.description:
Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) closed out Friday's session with a 21% jump, adding to its strong rally since coming public, after Seaport Global gave the stablecoin issuer its first Buy rating, calling it a "top-tier crypto 'disruptor' with a sizeable future opportunity."

The intraday climb gives leaves CRCL with a ~115% surge over the last five trading sessions. The stock has gained some eight-fold since its market debut in early June.

The upbeat sell-side coverage follows the U.S. Senate's move this week to pass a bill that would create a regulatory framework for stablecoins.

"On the back of an improving regulatory climate, we expect adoption globally of stablecoins such as USDC,Circle's flagship product, grows rapidly from here - we see the stablecoin 'market cap' potentially reaching $2T over the longer-term, from roughly $260B today," analyst Jeff Cantwell wrote in a note to clients.

By the end of next year, the total stablecoin market cap is expected to reach $500B, he noted.

As such, Cantwell expects Circle's (NYSE:CRCL) revenue to grow 25%-30% on an annual basis, with the "abilityto drive operating leverage as it scales further."

data.title: New York City sets minimum pay for rideshare drivers
data.description:
New York City drivers for Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) will get a minimum pay hike of 5% under a new set of rules established by the city’s Taxi and Limousine Commission (“TLC”).

The city agreed to a lower rate than the originally proposed 6.1% following pushback from Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT), which argued that a higher minimum wage would lead to increased fares for riders.

The pay increase is part of a set of new rules protecting rideshare drivers from being locked out of the app during periods of low demand. Rideshare companies must also give drivers 72 hours notice before being locked out.  

In an effort to keep costs down, Uber (NYSE:UBER) and Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) began locking drivers out during non-peak hours to avoid compensation for time spent driving to the next pickup and time spent waiting for the next passenger. According to Bloomberg, the lockout periods extended into all hours, not just non-peak hours, “causing financial and mental stress for drivers, most of whom work full-time.”

This prompted the TLC to close the regulatory loophole that allowed for rideshare lockout periods that artificially inflated the utilization rate…fewer drivers meant higher utilization rates.

“While these changes are a step in the right direction, we still have concerns that the underlying pay formula will still deprive drivers of earning opportunities, drive up prices for riders and reduce ride availability, which isn’t good for anyone — especially the drivers who depend on steady demand to make a living,” Lyft spokesperson CJ Macklin was quoted as saying by Bloomberg.

Shares of both Uber (UBER) and Lyft (LYFT) reacted negatively to the news, but have recouped some of their earlier losses.

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs edge lower for eighth consecutive week
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. lost 1 to 554, again reaching their lowest total since November 2021 and falling for the eighth straight week, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest survey.

The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. dipped by 1 to 438 this week, and gas rigs shed 2 to 111, and 5 rigs were classified as miscellaneous.

Baker Hughes said this week's decline dropped the total count by 34 rigs, or slightly less than 6%, compared to last year at this time.

Texas had the largest decline this week, down 4 units to 258 rigs working, and Oklahoma dropped 2 units to end the week at 47 rigs.

Canada's total rig count increased by 1 unit to 139, down 27 units, or 16%, from the same period a year ago.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow finished mostly lower as markets seesawed back and forth
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages seesawed back and forth on Friday and finished mixed as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July.

At the close of trading the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.22%, as it ended at 5,967.84. Furthermore, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.08% as it closed at 42,206.82 and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.51%. as it concluded at 19,447.41.

Across the S&P 500, five of the 11 sectors rounded out the day in the green, with Consumer Staples and Energy posting the strongest gains while Communication Services was the weakest performer so far.

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower, with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) sliding 3 basis points to 3.90% while the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved down 1 basis point to 4.38%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire.

Waller said on Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

“This week the only things that the market had to deal with were escalating conflict in the Middle East, a spiking oil price, ongoing uncertainty regarding trade policy, a shortened trading week, oh, and also triple witching.  Apart from that, it was a quiet one,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

With all that being said, King also added: “We believe new all time highs will be made this year.”

On the geopolitical front President Donald Trump announced that he will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

From an economic vantage point, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus. Furthermore, the U.S. Leading Indicator figures inched down 0.1% to 99.0 in May, matching the consensus estimates.

data.title: Chart Industries tapped as Top Pick by Morgan Stanley after Flowserve merger
data.description:
Chart Industries (NYSE:GTLS) was named as a Top Pick by Morgan Stanley analysts Friday following its $19B all-stock merger with Flowserve (FLS), which the bank said should further boost what already was a compelling growth and relative valuation story for the shares.

The deal pulls accelerates Chart's (NYSE:GTLS) initiatives such as A/M expansion, free cash flow durability, and delevering, but regardless, Morgan Stanley analyst Daniel Kutz said he views the company's organic growth prospects as "compelling, and deal-related upside as a potential incremental valuation boost."

Deal synergy targets are compelling if realized, particularly the guided commercial synergy target of a 200 bps top line boost to compound annual growth rate, according to Kutz, but even as some question whether such a target is achievable, Chart's (GTLS) organic growth prospects already are compelling; if the company come close to the growth targets, the shares are "meaningfully" undervalued, the analyst said.

In light of the merger, and including confirmation that the new company would retain Flowserve's (FLS) dividend - already at ~30% of Flowserve's standalone free cash flow -  plus the stronger liquidity position of the pro forma entity, Kutz now assumes the merged company's total shareholder return percentage FCF "would be ~50%+ of FCF immediately post transaction close, comprised of both dividends and share buybacks."

data.title: Lilly, Novo, and Amgen set for key obesity trial readouts at upcoming ADA event
data.description:
Leading weight loss drug developers Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) are scheduled to present major clinical trial readouts at the American Diabetes Association's (ADA) 85th Scientific Sessions taking place June 20-23 in Chicago.

Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is set to post results for its investigational oral weight loss therapy, orforglipron, from its ACHIEVE-1 Phase 3 trial for adults with type 2 diabetes at an ADA-sponsored symposium on June 21.

The Indiana-based drugmaker released topline data from ACHIEVE-1 in April, noting that the global trial, the first of seven Phase 3 studies for orforglipron, reached the main goal as the once-daily pill cut patients’ weight by ~8% on average over 40 weeks.

Novo Nordisk (NVO) is also set for a symposium presentation to release data from its REDEFINE 1 and REDEFINE 2 Phase 3 trials for its next-gen weight loss therapy, CagriSema on June 22.

Shares of the Danish drugmaker plunged in December after the once-weekly injectable fell short of the company’s initial expectations in its REDEFINE 1 Phase 3 trial, which enrolled obese or overweight adults with one or more associated illnesses.

“The presentation of the CagriSema REDEFINE 1 and 2 trials are the first ever Phase 3 data presented on a GLP-1 and amylin receptor agonist combination, offering insights into the potential of this investigational medicine,” the company said ahead of the event.

Meanwhile, the safety of Amgen’s (NASDAQ:AMGN) experimental weight loss therapy MariTide will be in focus when the Thousand Oaks, California-based firm shares full data from a Phase 2 study for the injectable on June 23.

Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) shares fell in November after the company reported that MariTide caused an average weight loss of up to 20% over 52 weeks. in the trial, which enrolled individuals with obesity or overweight conditions but without Type 2 diabetes.

Previously, a report from Cantor Fitzgerald indicated that a potential loss of bone mineral density could be a safety risk linked to MariTide, which targets gut hormone receptors (GLP-1) as well as glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide receptors.

Other notable weight loss drug developers selected for ADA presentations include Altimmune (ALT), Metsera (MTSR), and Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX).
